01

Scaling One Banking Experience Across 39 Institutions

Lead Visual / UX Designer  ·  Consumer & Retail Banking  ·  Multi-Brand Platform  ·  Figma

Design System Multi-Brand Accessibility Component Architecture iOS & Android Desktop & Tablet
Project Overview
A digital banking platform serving 39 financial institutions across desktop, tablet, iOS, and Android — each with its own brand identity, built on one underlying product. The challenge was enabling every institution to feel distinct to its customers while keeping the experience consistent, accessible, and sustainable for an agile engineering team to ship at pace.
My Role
Led the shift from one-off per-brand design work to a shared component system with branding as a configurable layer — giving every institution the same UX logic and accessibility baseline by default. Documented the system end-to-end so the approach could scale beyond my own execution.
Challenge
Each of the 39 institutions required a distinct brand presence, but individual per-brand design work was unsustainable — creating inconsistencies across the product, bottlenecks in delivery, and accessibility standards at risk of slipping under release pressure.
Solution
Designed a token-driven component system where branding (colour, typography, logo) was a configurable layer on top of shared UX logic. Proposed a phased rollout approach for accessibility: ship fast, then follow with a compliance pass — protecting both the timeline and the quality standard.
Key Design Decisions
Separated brand identity from interaction logic at the component level — no new brand required a design rebuild. Accessibility treated as a system-level baseline, not a per-project checklist. Comprehensive system documentation ensured brand onboarding could scale without a single point of failure on any one designer.
39
Financial institution brands on one consistent product
4
Platforms — desktop, tablet, iOS, Android — from one component system
A11y ✓
Accessibility compliance maintained without delaying releases
Scalable
Documented system removed single point of failure in brand onboarding
Design Samples
Desktop — the shared dashboard experience adapted across institutions, shown on MacBook.
Tablet — the same component system rendering the full dashboard on iPad, confirming cross-device consistency.
Information Architecture — full app structure across pre-login, dashboard, activity, move money, products, and settings, used to align product and engineering on scope before design began.
User Flow — Account Management journey from app entry through account details, transaction filtering, and all edge and error states.
Feature Flow — Pay Bills (Flows 1 & 2): one-time payment and recurring payment setup, covering 14 screens and 4 user flows.
Feature Flow — Pay Bills (Flows 3 & 4): manage scheduled payments and supporting screens including add payee and destructive delete confirmation.
Screen Inventory — full scope of mobile screens delivered across all features, illustrating the depth and consistency of the component system at scale.
02

Field Investigation Application

Loan Officer Tool  ·  Web Application  ·  Banking & Financial Services

Design Lead User Flow Wireframes Component Library Visual Design Style Guide
Project Overview
A purpose-built digital tool for loan officers to initiate, process, and verify loan applications through field investigations. The application consolidates applicant profiling, document verification, family tree mapping, and enquiry tracking into a single structured workflow — replacing fragmented paper-based processes across field teams.
My Role
Led end-to-end design as sole design owner — owning design direction, user flow architecture, wireframes, style guide, component library, and final visual designs. Collaborated with product and engineering stakeholders to align decisions with technical and compliance constraints.
Challenge
Loan officers were managing field investigations using paper forms and disconnected tools, creating bottlenecks in data collection, review, and approval. The process lacked traceability, was prone to errors, and made it difficult for operations to track enquiry status in real time.
Solution
Designed a structured FI workflow covering a status dashboard for enquiry-level tracking, multi-applicant detail forms (main + sub-applicants), a customer verification module with identity and profile steps, and a family tree builder for joint loan scenarios — all built on a consistent, accessible component system.
Key Design Decisions
Progressive disclosure in structured forms to reduce cognitive load for field officers. Clear status indicators for at-a-glance enquiry tracking. Tabbed navigation between applicant profiles to minimise context switching. Designed for data-dense workflows while maintaining clarity and low training overhead.
Unified
Single platform for the full FI lifecycle — initiation to verification
↓ Errors
Structured forms reduced data entry inconsistencies across field teams
Real-time
Dashboard visibility into enquiry status for operations teams
Scalable
Component system built to support future modules without redesign
Design Samples
User Flow — Customer onboarding journey from FI Dashboard landing through profile selection, global constitution, and business registration to completion.
UI Screens — Dashboard status states (New, Progress, Submitted) alongside enquiry detail views, showing how field officers track and manage loan applications at a glance.
Style Guide — Colour palette, typography scale (Roboto), spacing system, and layout grid defined to ensure visual consistency across all screens.
Component Library — Buttons, search/text fields, icons, and selection controls documented across all states (default, active, filled, disabled) for development handoff.
03

Global Bank Mobile App — APAC Revamp

UX Designer  ·  Global Consumer Banking  ·  APAC Region  ·  Sketch & OmniGraffle

UX Design Usability Testing Interaction Design User Flow iOS & Mobile Desktop & Tablet Multi-Region
Project Overview
A large-scale revamp of a major global bank's mobile application for the APAC region, covering 12+ banking features across two delivery phases. In parallel, contributed to the Payments & Transfers desktop feature across multiple regions — including the Add Payee function — and worked on approximately 35 complete banking features for desktop and tablet across multiple regions.
My Role
UX Designer embedded across two major workstreams: the APAC mobile revamp and the global desktop P&T feature build. Responsible for usability testing, user flow analysis, visual mockups (Sketch), interaction documentation, and production-ready asset & product guide delivery for development handoff.
Challenge
The existing APAC mobile experience was inconsistent across banking features and did not meet the quality standard of the bank's global product standard. For the desktop P&T builds, the same Payments & Transfers feature had to work across three distinct regional contexts — each with different regulatory, language, and product requirements.
Solution
For the mobile revamp: conducted usability testing to identify friction points, then redesigned user flows and produced annotated interaction documents and visual mockups per feature. For the desktop build: designed region-adapted user flows and wireframes for the Add Payee function using OmniGraffle, ensuring each regional variant met local product and compliance requirements while sharing a consistent interaction model.
Key Design Decisions
Structured each feature with user journey maps, interaction annotations, and error state documentation to give engineering teams unambiguous handoff material. Transfer type selection (IMPS vs NEFT) surfaced with clear contextual descriptions to reduce user error on high-stakes transactions. Beneficiary flows designed to support both registered and unregistered payees with dual input modes (Account + IFSC or Mobile + MMID).
12+
Banking features redesigned across two delivery phases for APAC mobile
35+
Global Bank features delivered for desktop & tablet across multiple regions
3
Regional variants adapted per local regulatory and product requirements
Global
End-to-end design delivery: research, flows, UI, interaction docs & dev assets
Design Samples
User Journey — Payments & Transfers: end-to-end journey from login through transfer type selection, payment details, pre-confirmation, and confirmation, with annotated decision points at each step.
User Flow — Online Account Opening: 3-step application process covering state selection, identity verification, e-signature, co-applicant paths, and account funding, with edge cases and regional notes documented.
Interaction Annotations — IMPS Transaction: transfer mode selection through amount entry, remarks, pre-confirmation, and success state, each screen annotated with interaction triggers and behaviour guidance for development handoff.
Wireframe Spec — Fund Your Account: step 3 of the account opening application, with numbered element annotations mapping each UI component to its behaviour, validation rules, and conditional logic.
